applicable_regime.explanation: Accrual date 2026-04-13 ≥ 2023-03-24 → HB 837 modified-comparative 51% bar applies (§768.81(6)).

fact_pattern_anchor.controlling_authority: Birge v. Charron, 107 So. 3d 350 (Fla. 2012); Pierce v. Progressive Am. Ins. Co.; Eppler v. Tarmac

doctrine_gates_applied.effect: Modified-comparative 51% bar for non-medical-negligence accruing after 2023-03-24.
Non-party fault apportioned if factually supported AND pled per Rule 1.110(d).
doctrine_gates_applied.statute_or_case_cite: Fabre v. Marin, 623 So. 2d 1182 (Fla. 1993); Nash v. Wells Fargo

doctrine_gates_applied.effect: Rear driver presumed at fault unless four named rebuttals evidenced.
doctrine_gates_applied.statute_or_case_cite: Birge v. Charron; Pierce; Eppler; Douglas-Seibert

rationale.net_apportionment_walk:
Start: rear_end anchor = 95% (rear_driver → vehicle_1_driver_insured).
  claimant_more_fault: 22.5 pts (police_report_narrative (hard_data) — DOC-009-INCIDENT)
  claimant_more_fault: 22.5 pts (police_report_field (hard_data) — DOC-009-INCIDENT)
  claimant_more_fault: 22.5 pts (citation_issued (hard_data) — DOC-009-INCIDENT)
  claimant_more_fault: 7.5 pts (party_admission (rebuttable_signal) — DOC-009-SUBRO)
  doctrine: hb_837_51_bar — §768.81(6); HB 837 (eff. 2023-03-24)
  doctrine: fabre_apportionment — Fabre v. Marin, 623 So. 2d 1182 (Fla. 1993); Nash v. Wells Fargo
  doctrine: rear_end_rebuttable_presumption — Birge v. Charron; Pierce; Eppler; Douglas-Seibert
  doctrine: joint_several_abolished — §768.81(3)
Net per party: vehicle_1_driver_insured=20.00%, pinnacle_steel_works_owner=1.67%, vehicle_2_driver_brightway=76.67%, brightway_freight_carriers=1.67%

basis_evidence.quoted_span: Vehicle 1 (insured) was fully stopped, waiting to enter the yard, when Vehicle 2 struck it from the rear. Vehicle 1 took no evasive action because none was available; it was stationary and lawfully stopped.
Primary collision factor: Vehicle 2 — failure to maintain a safe following distance. No contributing factor attributed to Vehicle 1.
Citation: the Vehicle 2 driver was cited.
Belted driver of a box truck struck on the driver side by a tractor-trailer while stopped. Significant intrusion to the cab.

Left shoulder deformity, cervical tenderness, seatbelt bruising. Imaging: left clavicle fracture confirmed on X-ray; cervical MRI shows C5-C6 disc protrusion. Displaced clavicle fracture — orthopedic fixation scheduled; cervical injury managed conservatively with PT. Medical specials to date: $38,500 (ED, imaging, orthopedic surgery estimate, physical therapy). Permanent impairment likely given operative fracture.

change_conditions: If Birge-category rebuttal evidence lands (mechanical failure / sudden stop / lane-change-by-lead / illegal-stop), rear-driver anchor shifts down materially.
Demand on file — sufficient-evidence assessment is the gating next decision; new evidence may flip the §624.155(4) call.
